# Figure description: Figure_07.png

**Source**: `usgs_teniz_copper_bundle/descriptions/Figure_07.png` (PDF page 28)  
**Generated by**: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5

---

## Figure Type
This is an index map showing the spatial distribution of geological and mineral resource map sheets, prospects, seismic survey lines, and sedimentary cover extent within the study area.

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers the Teniz Basin in northern Kazakhstan, spanning approximately 66°E to 72°E longitude and 50°N to 52°N latitude. The city of Astana is marked in the eastern portion of the map. The figure shows the basin extent (shown in light gray) and Cenozoic unconsolidated sedimentary cover (shown with stippled pattern in yellow/orange outline). An inset map in the lower right shows the regional context within Kazakhstan, bordered by Russia to the north and China to the east, with the study area indicated by a small rectangle in north-central Kazakhstan.

## Key Content
The main map displays:
- Light gray shading representing the Teniz Basin extent
- Yellow/orange outlined areas with stippled pattern showing Cenozoic unconsolidated sedimentary cover
- Multiple blue rectangles labeled as mineral resource map sheets from the Uluatu-Kokchetav Series at 1:200,000 scale, including: Sheet M-42-II, Sheet N-42-XXXIV, Sheet M-42-III, Sheet M-42-IX, Sheet M-42-VIII, Sheet M-42-XIV, and Sheet M-42-XV
- Numerous small white squares representing prospect locations, concentrated in the central and western portions of the basin
- Gray diagonal lines representing seismic survey lines, forming a dense network particularly in the central basin area
- Scale bars showing 0-140 kilometers and 0-70 miles
- Projection information: Asia North Albers Equal Area Projection, Central meridian 69°E, latitude of origin 30°N

## Text and Labels
**Map title area:**
"Sandstone Copper Assessment of the Teniz Basin, Kazakhstan"

**Legend (EXPLANATION):**
- Teniz Basin [gray box]
- Cenozoic unconsolidated sedimentary cover [stippled box with yellow outline]
- Sheet XXX | Mineral resources of USSR—map sheet identification: Uluatu-Kokchetav Series, scale 1:200,000 [blue box]
- Seismic survey line [diagonal line symbol]
- Prospect [white square symbol]

**Map labels:**
- Sheet labels: "Sheet M-42-II", "Sheet N-42-XXXIV", "Sheet M-42-III", "Sheet M-42-IX", "Sheet M-42-VIII", "Sheet M-42-XIV", "Sheet M-42-XV"
- Geographic label: "Astana"
- Coordinates marked at edges: 66°E, 69°E, 72°E (longitude); 50°N, 52°N (latitude)

**Caption:**
"Figure 7. Map showing location of 1:200,000-scale geological and mineral resource map sheets used in this study relative to extent of Teniz Basin, Kazakhstan (Fugro Robertson, Ltd., 2008), extent of Cenozoic cover (Syusyura and others, 2010), location of prospects, and seismic survey lines (Syusyura and others, 2010)."

**Inset map labels:**
RUSSIA, KAZAKHSTAN, CHINA, with coordinates 60°E, 100°E, 60°N, 45°N

## Research Value
This figure provides essential context for the mineral resource assessment by showing the spatial framework of available geological data, including Soviet-era mineral resource map sheets, existing prospect locations, and geophysical survey coverage. The distribution of prospects clustered in areas with Cenozoic cover and dense seismic survey coverage indicates where exploration has been concentrated and helps researchers understand data availability and exploration history. The figure enables researchers to locate specific map sheets referenced in the assessment and understand the relationship between sedimentary cover extent and the distribution of known prospects, which is critical for evaluating permissive tracts for sandstone-hosted copper mineralization.
